mnemonics.title: Response Time Components
mnemonics.content: R-E-S-P: Response = Service + Queue + Processing + Delay (network)

mnemonics.title: Capacity Planning Metrics
mnemonics.content: TULS: Throughput, Utilization, Latency, Saturation point - the four pillars of measurement

acronyms.usage: Measures system throughput for transactional workloads like databases
Contract defining performance expectations between provider and customer
Metrics tied to business goals for measuring planning success
Reliability metric important for capacity and availability planning
Framework for measuring and guaranteeing performance levels
Maximum data loss tolerance, affects backup capacity planning

visualTips.visualization: Draw an S-curve: flat at low utilization, linear in middle, steep at saturation point - the 'knee' marks capacity limit
Imagine a checkout line: empty = fast service, long line = exponential wait times increase
Three horizontal lines: green (good), yellow (warning), red (critical) - current performance compared to baseline zones

analogies.analogy: Throughput is like highway capacity (cars per hour); latency is like travel time from point A to B
A four-lane highway narrowing to one lane - overall speed limited by the narrow section
A sponge: can absorb water (workload) up to a point, then starts dripping (performance drop)
